customer_id.business_reasoning: Customer ID is the primary key for the record. Any row missing it cannot be matched, deduplicated, or trusted in downstream analysis.

name.business_reasoning: Customer name is needed for human review, account lookup, and support workflows. Filling it artificially would create a misleading customer identity.
name.risk_assessment: Low - incomplete identity records are removed instead of guessed

email.business_reasoning: Email is required for outreach, verification, and retention campaigns. Records without email cannot support customer communication.
email.risk_assessment: Low - only incomplete contact records are removed

amount.business_reasoning: Median purchase amount represents the typical transaction without being skewed by high-value outliers.
amount.risk_assessment: Low - median is stable and resistant to skew

category.business_reasoning: The most common category is a defensible default for a low-cardinality business segment field.
category.risk_assessment: Medium - mode assumes missing records follow the dominant group

last_updated.interpretation: Assumes the last known timestamp remains valid until updated
last_updated.business_reasoning: For time-series feeds, forward fill preserves temporal continuity when later observations inherit the prior state.
last_updated.risk_assessment: Medium - only suitable where values change slowly over time
